reviews.review_description: As an experiment in VR, this is a very cool and very well-done VR experience. Visually, it’s very captivating. If you’re expecting a game experience, you’ll be disappointed because it’s not much of one. I’m giving it 4 stars for what it is and am excited to try other titles by the developer!
Super neat concept ideal for VR
Great concept. I really liked the movement system and the art style.  Looking forward to seeing more story and clues.  Overall great concept.

After reading the other reviews and seeing the feedback from creator of the game. This game lives up to its idea well. Keep your graphic artist. The whole color scheme with the era you choose went well together. There was times when I had to stop and take in the view because it looked so well together.

developer_response.body: Hi Eldowden, thanks for your detailed and professional feedback, that help us to improve that demo. Please, take a moment to play our another demo "Rock & Rails" and give us feedback.

reviews.review_description: Game won't move past beginning cut scene

reviews.review_title: A great art project and concept!
reviews.review_description: But let's be honest here it's not a game. It's not something I would even pay money for. It's reminds me alot of the early 90's PC click on everything style game. It just don't work in this day and age. But if you look at it not as a game it's very impressive outlandish art that's been forgotten.

By no means a finished product or a fulfilling experience. But the potential for an awesome detective like game is there. A definite buy if the devs create a more engaging story in which reading the clues can actually help lead us to the killer.
I guessed every person and still never won. What's the point.

developer_response.body: Thanks OttoLehto for you feedback, that is important for us improve that demo. We appreciate if you take a moment to play our another demo, "Rock & Rails".

reviews.review_description: Could of had so much potential..  you have tonfigure out who poisoned the guy.  I found him in less then 1o seconds and the game was over...  it doesnt change killers sonthere is zero replayablility.  Complete waste of storage and 8 seconds of my life I will never get back

The visual style is amazing and I would love a full blown app or game set in fancy ball/party setting with this art style.

The rest isn't good however, you,really given a short time to guess the killer. There are 0 clues so you literally have to accuse everyone you can interact with to find the correct one. The killer is always the same do there's zero replay value at all.

Spoiler of killer whereabouts:

The killer can be easy to miss. You can find the killer hiding behind the staircase to the right.

developer_response.body: Hi lakuza, thanks a lot for your feedback. Our idea was to put this concept available for everyone so we could validate the potential to turn Rococo into a full project in the future. Thanks again for the great feedback.

reviews.review_description: Great graphic. The timer is annoying. Need more time to ponder and investigate.

I like the concept, but this feels incomplete, not because of the length, but due to the lack of depth. Great visual style and concept, but literally  8 taps and then the game is done.  If this is expanded into a full blown who-done-it cluedo game, then it would definitely be worth purchasing
The women in this have really nice racks, you should develop a game around that.  Do away with the timer...because it keeps me from looking at nice racks as long as I want. Thanks.
As other reviews have said; there is no strategy  or thought needed to find the killer. You just go around, blame everyone and hope for the best.

reviews.review_title: Very early demo from Black River Studios
reviews.review_description: 2019 finds us with a pretty substantial catalogue of VR titles from Black River Studios through the years, each bringing something wholly unique to the table. How cool is it then to look back on a demo from their early days; indeed one of their earliest apps of record in the Oculus store. I’m sure at the time, with no context as to who Black River would become or what purpose this served, this app came across as a stinker. Looking back at older reviews, we can see the polarizing opinions. Many dismissed the lack of depth, while others praised the notable art style. If these comments sound familiar, that’s because they are. This divide has consistently been their MO. The studio created this demo largely as a filler between major projects to see if this was worth pursuing. We of course know now they did not develop this into a full game. Who knows if this would have been successful? We can see tons of potential. The art style is unlike anything seen in VR. The atmosphere would have been perfect for a full-fledged Clue-like mystery. The studio’s strengths have always been in embracing and tackling numerous genres, styles, and timbres, thus ensuring no two experiences would be alike. We can only judge the merits of what little gameplay exists here, which isn’t much and which isn’t really gameplay. You have a character who is poisoned, and you explore a Victorian ballroom finding the killer. I’m no genius, but I discovered the killer in approximately 3-4 minutes. Therefore, the whole thing was a bit deflating for me. I found the experience more satisfying as an early insight into Black River’s evolution rather than in the content as a stand-alone VR piece. Context is everything here. On its own, it’s barely a 2-Star app which rides largely on the strength of its art style. But with hindsight, it benefits from the knowledge that this was a precursor of better things to come. That doesn’t exactly qualify it as “good”, but compelling nonetheless.
